notes: RE-ASSESS after Control fix (5e22e47c). backchannel-observe for mistral-vibe. Vibe renders a TUI-only tool-permission dialog (approval_app Textual widget, an in-memory asyncio.Future never written to messages.jsonl) — exactly the transcript-invisible waiting state read-back (issue #732) targets. The read-back path is reader.CaptureScreen -> DetectUI -> TerminalObserver -> SessionDetector, gated on control consent at terminal_observer.go:111. Commit 5e22e47c declared Control{SupportsInput}+ControlPermission on vibe/agent.go (consent gate resolved). RE-RECORDED 2026-07-13 against irrlichd 0.5.7+62118c4 (issue #988 worktree, which adds vibe's dialog markers 'Permission for the' / 'Allow for remainder of this session' to uidetect.go's trustDialogMarkers): MARKER FIX CONFIRMED CORRECT — the captured on-screen text exactly matches ('Permission for the bash tool (touch *)' title; 'Allow for remainder of this session' option), and events.jsonl shows ui_detected 'trust dialog detected (terminal read-back)' immediately followed by a real ready->waiting state_transition, then 'trust dialog cleared' -> waiting->working once the driver answers with Enter. DetectUI genuinely recognizes vibe's dialog now. HOWEVER a DISTINCT, NEWLY-EXPOSED blocker keeps this known_failing: the waiting/working edge fires on the TRANSIENT PRESESSION identity (proc-<PID>, born ready at launch) rather than the reconciled real session_<ts>_<hash> id. mistral-vibe's presession->real-session reconciliation (the same proc-<PID>-then-session_<id> pattern used elsewhere in this matrix) takes ~13s in this recording; the trust dialog appeared at +3s (well before reconciliation) and was dismissed at +14s (just AFTER reconciliation), but the daemon's terminal-observer/backchannel per-session bookkeeping (keyed by session_id) never re-keys from the presession's id to the real session's id, so the whole waiting arc stays attached to proc-<PID> — which is discarded (transcript_removed / presession_removed) once the real session takes over. The spec anchors ui_dialog_waiting/dialog_dismissed same_session_as: session_birth (the reconciled real session), so the matcher finds no waiting transition there. 4/7 phases pass (presession_birth, session_birth, pid_bind, turn_start); ui_dialog_waiting fails ('no event matching waiting found for the real session'), cascading dialog_dismissed/settle. This is a presession-identity-continuity gap in the terminal-observer/backchannel path (distinct from, and exposed only after, the marker fix) — worth a follow-up issue. daemon=bug persists; known_failing stays true.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
